name: Sigyn Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Sigyn Therapeutics develops medical devices to treat cancer and infectious disease disorders. Its lead therapeutic candidate is Sigyn Therapy, designed to extract pathogens and toxins from the bloodstream. The company also has device platforms to optimize cancer therapies, including ImmunePrep, ChemoPrep, and ChemoPure.

exposures.sentence: Sigyn Therapeutics, Inc. (Sigyn, the Company, we, us, or our) develops medical devices to treat cancer and infectious disease disorders.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 550,000 ESRD patients in the US
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 550,000 individuals suffer from ESRD in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: $1 billion added revenue per month of extended life for each dialysis giant
tamClaims.sentence: every month of extended life would equate to approximately $1 billion in added revenues for each company.

tamClaims.claim: 90,000 on kidney transplant waitlist, <30,000 transplants annually
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, approximately 90,000 individuals are on the waitlist for a kidney transplant, yet fewer than 30,000 kidney transplants are performed each year.

tamClaims.claim: 3 million antibiotic-resistant infections, 35,000 deaths annually in US
tamClaims.sentence: According to the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), nearly three million individuals are infected with antibiotic resistant bacterial infections in the U.S. each year, which results in more than 35,000 deaths.

tamClaims.claim: 48.9 million sepsis cases, 11 million deaths globally in 2017
tamClaims.sentence: a report entitled; Global, Regional, and National Sepsis Incidence and Mortality, 1990-2017: Analysis for the Global Burden of Disease Study, reported 48.9 million cases of sepsis and 11 million deaths in 2017.

tamClaims.claim: US sepsis financial burden exceeds $24 billion
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, sepsis was reported to be the most common cause of hospital deaths with an annual financial burden that exceeds $24 billion.

tamClaims.claim: 85 million dialysis treatments annually in US
tamClaims.sentence: more than 550,000 individuals have ESRD, which results in approximately 85 million kidney dialysis treatments being administered in the United States each year.

tamClaims.claim: 5 million antimicrobial resistance deaths in 2019, projected 10 million by 2050
tamClaims.sentence: The United Nations reported approximately 5 million deaths in 2019 were associated with antimicrobial drug resistance and projects the annual death toll could increase to 10 million by 2050 in the absence of new therapeutic advances.
